title: dryopsis ferruginea
intro: Dryopsis ferruginea, also known as the rusty fern, is a type of evergreen fern native to the southeastern United States and parts of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Dryopsis ferruginea include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Dryopsis ferruginea is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
